## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/features/sync/repository/sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart` is **1269 lines**, exceeding the 1000-line hard limit from AGENTS.md. Split along the natural group boundaries already present:
  - `sync_maintenance_repository_original_test.dart` ‚Äî the "Original Tests" group (syncMeasurables, syncCategories, syncDashboards, syncHabits, syncAiSettings; lines 126‚Äì532)
  - `sync_maintenance_repository_labels_test.dart` ‚Äî the "Labels" group (lines 534‚Äì611)
  - `sync_maintenance_repository_logging_test.dart` ‚Äî the "Logging Tests" group (lines 613‚Äì699)
  - `sync_maintenance_repository_progress_test.dart` ‚Äî `fetchTotalsForSteps`, `_runOperation` progress callbacks, `syncAgentEntities`, `syncAgentLinks`, `backfillAgentEntityClocks`, `backfillAgentLinkClocks` (lines 701‚Äì1269)
  Each split file mirrors the source and stays well under 400 lines.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the proposed files would all mirror the same single source (`sync_maintenance_repository.dart`), which the one-test-file-per-source rule forbids; the four group boundaries already provide the navigation structure the split was after.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/features/sync/repository/sync_maintenance_repository.dart` is 582 lines. The `_syncDomainFor` / `_totalsDomainFor` switch pair (lines 516‚Äì568) adds ~55 lines of routing boilerplate that could be folded into the `SyncOperation` struct itself (store the two strings at construction time). Doing so would shrink the impl file by ~50 lines and remove two large switch statements.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the two ~27-line switches are replaced by a const `_syncDomainByStep` map (the names are irregular: backfill steps keep bare method names) and a derived `'fetchTotals_${step.name}'` slug for totals; `complete` keeps its UnsupportedError contract. ~30 lines saved.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart` lines 25‚Äì55: defines four inline `Fake` classes ‚Äî `FakeMeasurableDataType`, `FakeCategoryDefinition`, `FakeDashboardDefinition`, `FakeHabitDefinition` ‚Äî each with `id` and `deletedAt` fields. `FakeCategoryDefinition`, `FakeDashboardDefinition`, and `FakeHabitDefinition` already exist in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` (lines 523‚Äì527) without `id`/`deletedAt` fields. `FakeMeasurableDataType` is absent from the central file entirely. The inline definitions shadow the central ones and violate the centralized-mocks rule. **Resolution:** add `id` and `deletedAt` to the three existing central `Fake` classes, add `FakeMeasurableDataType` with those fields to `test/mocks/mocks.dart`, then remove the four inline definitions and import from the central file. **RESOLVED:** the central `FakeCategoryDefinition`/`FakeDashboardDefinition`/`FakeHabitDefinition` in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` now carry optional `id`/`deletedAt` fields (with defaults so existing no-arg callers keep working), `FakeMeasurableDataType` was added there, and the four inline shadow definitions were deleted.

- [x] **[HIGH]** Copy-paste permutation: the 4 sync methods (`syncMeasurables`, `syncCategories`, `syncDashboards`, `syncHabits`) each have two tests ‚Äî `'enqueues X for sync'` and `'skips deleted X'` ‚Äî with identical structure (stub DB call, stub enqueue, call sync method, capture, assert length, assert id, assert status). These 8 tests span ~270 lines (lines 127‚Äì406) and differ only in the DB method name, entity type, and field accessor. Extract a parameterized helper: **RESOLVED:** the 8 tests are generated from a 4-entry `entitySyncSpecs` record list (name, stubRows, callSync, make) in a loop ‚Äî same coverage, one body per behavior, ~190 fewer duplicated lines. The captured-id extraction uses the `EntityDefinition` union `id` getter, dropping the per-type casts.
  ```dart
  Future<void> _testSyncEnqueues<T>({...}) async { ... }
  Future<void> _testSyncSkipsDeleted<T>({...}) async { ... }
  ```
  and call each helper once per entity type. This collapses ~270 lines to ~40.

- [x] **[MED]** `sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart` lines 502‚Äì531: `syncAiSettings` error test uses `expectLater(..., throwsA(exception))` but the three `getConfigsByType` stubs for `model` and `prompt` are set up even though they're unreachable when `inferenceProvider` throws. Remove the two unreachable stubs ‚Äî they add noise and could mask future behavior changes.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî the two unreachable stubs are removed with a comment explaining why: `thenThrow` fires synchronously while the `Future.wait` list literal is still being built, so the model/prompt fetches are never invoked.

- [x] **[LOW]** `sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart` lines 970‚Äì994: the `syncMaintenanceRepositoryProvider` test calls `getIt.reset()` inline and registers singletons before constructing a `ProviderContainer`. This is the same pattern as the centralized `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart`. Prefer using those helpers to avoid manual `addTearDown(getIt.reset)` bookkeeping.
  **RESOLVED:** the test now calls `setUpTestGetIt(additionalSetup: ...)` to register the only extra dependency the provider resolves from get_it (`VectorClockService`; `DomainLogger` comes from the helper) and `addTearDown(tearDownTestGetIt)` for cleanup, replacing the inline `getIt.reset()` + hand-registered singletons + `addTearDown(getIt.reset)` bookkeeping. The assertion is unchanged (provider builds a `SyncMaintenanceRepository`); using the real `DomainLogger` is harmless since construction never logs.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `SyncMaintenanceRepository._runOperation` progress math: the `onProgress` callback must always emit values in `[0.0, 1.0]` monotonically, and `onDetailedProgress` must satisfy `0 <= processed <= total`. A Glados property over generated `(List<bool> shouldSync, int total)` inputs would verify this invariant exhaustively without adding real DB calls ‚Äî wire stub fetchers that return generated lists of fictitious entities and verify the callback sequence.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added a Glados2 property (rows 0‚Äì32 √ó deletion mask, numRuns 120, `tags: 'glados'`) driving `syncMeasurables` with generated synced/skipped mixes: progress values all in [0,1], non-decreasing, ending at exactly 1.0; detailed counts keep `total` constant, `0 <= processed <= total`, non-decreasing, ending fully processed ‚Äî including the empty-list short-circuit.

- [x] **[LOW]** `fetchTotalsForSteps` with an arbitrary subset of `SyncStep` values: the function must never return a map with unexpected keys. A Glados property generating random `Set<SyncStep>` subsets and asserting that the returned map's key set equals the input set (excluding `SyncStep.complete`) would catch future regressions in the step-routing logic.
  **RESOLVED:** added a Glados property (`numRuns: 120`, `tags: 'glados'`) in the `fetchTotalsForSteps` group that generates random `List<SyncStep>` (length 0..N over `choose(SyncStep.values)`), collapses to a set, stubs every fetcher once, and asserts `totals.keys.toSet() == steps` exactly, all totals `>= 0`, and `complete` (when requested) contributes `0`. NOTE: the original review premise was slightly off ‚Äî the impl does **not** exclude `SyncStep.complete`; `_calculateTotalForStep` returns `0` for it and `fetchTotalsForSteps` keeps the key (confirmed by the existing `{SyncStep.complete: 0}` example test). The property therefore pins the actual contract: key set equals the input set, with `complete` included at `0`.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** Error paths for `syncAgentLinks`, `syncAgentEntities`, `backfillAgentEntityClocks`, and `backfillAgentLinkClocks` are never tested when the underlying repository throws. The existing "Logging Tests" group (lines 613‚Äì699) covers `syncMeasurables`, `syncCategories`, `syncDashboards`, `syncHabits` but is missing the four agent-related methods. Add `'should log and rethrow exception'` tests for each. **RESOLVED:** four spec-driven `'should log and rethrow exception when repository fails'` tests added ‚Äî `syncAgentEntities` (getAllEntities throws), `syncAgentLinks` (getAllLinks throws), `backfillAgentEntityClocks` (getEntitiesWithNullVectorClock throws), `backfillAgentLinkClocks` (getLinksWithNullVectorClock throws) ‚Äî each verifying the `LogDomain.sync` error log with the matching subDomain and the rethrow.

- [x] **[MED]** `syncLabels` has its error path untested ‚Äî the "Logging Tests" group does not include a `syncLabels` subgroup. Add a test matching the existing pattern.
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added the missing `syncLabels` logging group mirroring the others: `getAllLabelDefinitions` throws ‚Üí rethrown and logged under `subDomain: 'syncLabels'`.

- [x] **[MED]** `_calculateTotalForStep` with `SyncStep.complete` (should return 0 without calling any repository method) is exercised implicitly only when `fetchTotalsForSteps` is called with `complete` in the set. Add an explicit test that includes `SyncStep.complete` in the input set and asserts it is not present in the returned map (or returns 0).
  - **RESOLVED:** done ‚Äî added an explicit test: `fetchTotalsForSteps({SyncStep.complete})` returns `{complete: 0}` and touches no repository fetcher (verifyNever on all six).

- [x] **[LOW]** `SyncNodeProfileRepository.watchKnownNodes` test at line 206 uses `await pumpEventQueue()` to settle async stream emissions ‚Äî this is correct. However, the test makes 3 upsert calls and asserts `emissions.length == 3`. If the Drift `watchKnownNodes` stream coalesces rapid writes into fewer emissions on slow CI, the test could fail non-deterministically. Consider adding a small `await pumpEventQueue()` between each upsert to ensure ordering.
  **RESOLVED:** added `await pumpEventQueue()` after each of the three `upsertNode` calls in the `'emits the updated directory on upsert'` test. The repository broadcasts through a `StreamController.broadcast` whose `add` delivers on a microtask, so settling between writes makes the one-emission-per-upsert assertion deterministic regardless of scheduling on slow CI.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart`: each of the 8 copy-paste syncXxx tests (lines 127‚Äì406) constructs full mock stubs and calls `verify(capture(...))`. By DRY-ing these into helpers (see Test Quality section), the total mock-stub setup overhead is halved, saving ~16 stub registrations per run.
  - **RESOLVED:** (stale) ‚Äî the 8 copy-paste syncXxx tests were already DRY-ed into the parameterized `entitySyncSpecs` record loop (one stub/make/call spec per entity type, two shared test bodies).

- [x] **[LOW]** `sync_node_profile_repository_test.dart` Glados test at line 336 uses `numRuns: 60`. This is already conservative. The `pumpEventQueue()` at line 402 is called once per iteration and settles all pending Drift emissions ‚Äî this is correct and the only async cost in the Glados loop. No change needed.
  **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** confirmed accurate ‚Äî the observation itself concludes "No change needed," and that conclusion holds. `numRuns: 60` is an explicitly-chosen, non-default value (not the default 100), and the single per-iteration `pumpEventQueue()` is the correct and minimal way to settle the broadcast emissions inside the Glados loop. This is a pre-existing, well-tagged state-machine property unrelated to the items being actioned; no code change is warranted.

---

## Summary

- `sync_maintenance_repository_test.dart` is **oversized at 1269 lines** ‚Äî the single biggest issue; split into 4 files along existing group seams.
- 4 inline `Fake` entity classes duplicate or shadow centralized mock definitions.
- 8 copy-paste `syncXxx` test bodies (~270 lines) should be collapsed to parameterized helpers.
- 5 missing error-path tests: `syncLabels`, `syncAgentEntities`, `syncAgentLinks`, `backfillAgentEntityClocks`, `backfillAgentLinkClocks` throw paths.
- 1 medium Glados candidate: `_runOperation` progress invariants.
- `sync_node_profile_repository_test.dart` is a strong example: well-structured, Glados state-machine test correctly tagged, in-memory DB teardown clean.
